title: senecio viscosissimus
intro: Senecio viscosissimus, also known as sticky Jerusalem sage, is a perennial plant native to South Africa, characterized by its sticky leaves and clusters of small, yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Senecio viscosissimus include relief from skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Senecio viscosissimus is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically to the skin or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
